description_long: This factory building complex features a large, multi-story main structure with a series of stepped, rectangular volumes and a flat roof, flanked by two smaller brick buildings. The main building is constructed from light-colored concrete or stone, with minimal ornamentation and a utilitarian design, while the side buildings have a reddish-brown brick facade and regular window patterns. A long, narrow pier or conveyor extends from the main building over a body of water, suggesting an industrial waterfront setting. The windows are mostly small and rectangular, with some larger industrial-style windows on the side buildings. The complex sits on a raised concrete platform, with the water area occupying one side. The overall appearance is functional and robust, typical of early-to-mid 20th-century industrial architecture.
description: A large, utilitarian factory building complex with a stepped concrete main structure, two brick side buildings, and a pier extending over water.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stepped main building, two brick side buildings, and the pier extending over the water.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the main building's facade, the central pier, and the symmetrical arrangement of the side buildings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the stepped profile of the main building and the pier's attachment to the structure.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the plain back wall of the main building and the brick side buildings, with minimal window detail.
